question: Will Apple announce Vision Pro 2 (or any new Vision Pro hardware) at WWDC 2026 (June 8-12)?
textDescription: Apple WWDC 2026 runs June 8-12 in Cupertino. Polymarket prices 'Vision Pro 2 released before 2027' at 5% YES — Bloomberg/Mark Gurman reported Apple shelved the 2025 refresh, with high-end successor now eyed for 2027 amid sluggish original Vision Pro sales and excess inventory. RESOLVES YES if, during the June 8-12 WWDC keynote or session, Apple officially announces new Vision Pro hardware (Vision Pro 2, Vision Pro Lite, Apple Vision SE, or any other variant). Announcement must be from Apple stage or official channels, not leaks. RESOLVES NO if no new Vision Pro hardware is announced. Software updates to visionOS, accessories (strap, light seal), or third-party app reveals do NOT count — must be Apple-branded hardware.
newsShortReason: Apple has not announced any new Vision Pro hardware for WWDC 2026, and previous reports indicate delays in the Vision Pro 2 launch.
newsKeyEvidence: Apple's official announcement for WWDC 2026 focuses on software updates, including AI advancements and new developer tools, with no mention of new hardware releases. | Reports from April 2024 indicated that Apple does not plan to launch a second-generation Vision Pro headset until the end of 2026. | In December 2025, Apple postponed the Vision Pro 2 launch to late 2026 due to integration challenges with the M5 chip. | Despite low sales of the original Vision Pro, Apple continues to hire for its Vision Production Group, suggesting ongoing development in the mixed reality space. | Analysts have speculated that Apple may be shifting focus toward future AR products like smart glasses, with no official clarification from the company.
